key_risks: A dovish surprise at the June FOMC or cool May CPI could spark a tech/growth rebound, causing this defensive tilt to lag SPY.
Healthcare could give back its recent sharp outperformance on adverse MedPAC/policy headlines.
Persistent inflation prints could pressure rate-sensitive dividend and value names.
Short Treasury allocation caps upside if equities rally broadly.

portfolio.rationale: Strong recent momentum (+7.7% 30d, +5.6% 7d) as a defensive sector outperforming during broad market pullback; MedPAC report catalyst within window.
Defensive quality dividend tilt holding up well (+2.4% 30d) amid sticky inflation and recent equity weakness.
Value held up far better than growth during the tech-led selloff; sticky inflation and elevated rates favor value over growth.
Defense budget tailwind ($200B request) and geopolitical risk; steady positive momentum with low recent drawdown.
Cash-like ballast given hot CPI/PCE/PPI prints, FOMC on hold, and broad equity/tech drawdown risk into June CPI and FOMC.

portfolio_rationale: With sticky inflation (CPI 3.8% YoY, PPI +6% YoY), Fed on hold, and a sharp tech-led equity drawdown, I tilt defensively toward healthcare, dividends, and value while keeping short Treasury ballast for upside relative to SPY's high mega-cap tech exposure.
rationale_summary: Defensive, value-tilted equity allocation favoring sectors with positive recent momentum and lower drawdown risk into June CPI and the FOMC, with short-duration Treasury ballast.
